title: Customer Call — Orbit Systems Pilot Review (2026-08-05)
compiled_truth:
Call held 2026-08-05 to review the six-week pilot that [Orbit Systems](companies/orbit-systems) ran against their North American parcel-routing pipeline. Attendees: [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) (CEO, running point on enterprise deals at this stage), [Paz Santos](people/paz-santos) (engineering lead who built the integration shim), and [Dani Osei](people/dani-osei) from Orbit's infrastructure team.

Orbit had been evaluating us since late June after an intro through their VP of Platform. The pilot targeted their real-time shipment-manifest ingest layer — roughly 4 TB/day of structured event data flowing between regional hubs. Dani reported that during the pilot window they saw a consistent 38 % reduction in bandwidth between hub ingest nodes, with no increase in end-to-end latency. He was particularly impressed that the single-pass primitive handled authentication inline; their previous stack required a seperate verification step downstream which added ~20 ms per batch and occassionally caused ordering issues. That pain point alone had been responsible for two P1 incidents in Q2.

Seren walked through the proposed contract terms: $120K annual, covering up to 10 TB/day throughput across three deployment zones. Dani said the number was within his discretionary approval threshold, so legal review was the only remaining gate. He expected redlines back within ten business days. Paz flagged one open item — Orbit's hub in Montréal runs a slightly older kernel version that doesn't support our latest memory-mapped I/O path. She proposed shipping a compatibility layer by end of August, and Dani confirmed that timeline works since the Montréal hub isn't scheduled for production cutover until September anyway.

Tone of the call was very positive. Dani mentioned that two other teams inside [Orbit Systems](companies/orbit-systems) — customs document processing and fleet telematics — had asked about expanding the integration once the initial deployment stabilizes. Seren noted this internally as a potential upsell path into mid-six-figures by Q1 2027. [Paz Santos](people/paz-santos) agreed to send Dani the updated SDK docs and a draft runbook for the Montréal compatibility shim by Friday.

Overall this is our first real contract close and sets the template for how we run pilots going forward. Seren wants to formalize the six-week evaluation window as standard and create a lightweight ROI summary template based on the metrics Orbit tracked.
timeline: - **2026-06-22** | Intro call with Orbit Systems VP of Platform; initial interest in deterministic ingest pipeline.
- **2026-06-30** | Pilot scope agreed — North American parcel-routing manifest layer, ~4 TB/day.
- **2026-07-07** | [Paz Santos](people/paz-santos) ships integration shim for Orbit's hub infrastructure.
- **2026-07-14** | Pilot goes live across two of three Orbit deployment zones.
- **2026-08-05** | This call — pilot review with [Dani Osei](people/dani-osei); $120K contract terms presented.
- **2026-08-12** | Paz sends updated SDK docs and Montréal compatibility shim draft to Orbit.
- **2026-08-19** | Orbit legal returns redlines; minor indemnification language changes.
- **2026-08-26** | Contract signed — first paying customer. [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) announces internally.
- **2026-09-15** | Montréal hub cutover completed; all three [Orbit Systems](companies/orbit-systems) zones in production.
- **2027-01-10** | Expansion discussion kicks off covering customs doc processing and fleet telematics teams.

_facts.topic: Pilot review — Orbit Systems ($120K contract close)
